text: The 6 February 2023 Turkey–Syria earthquake occurred on 6 February 2023 at 04:17:32.1 (UTC) in Turkey and Syria, striking approximately 33 kilometres (21 miles) north-northwest of the city of Gaziantep, impacting ten provinces in Türkiye. This paper examines the overall response of the Ministry of National Education (MoNE) to the disaster, as a humanitarian organization and as an education provider. Applying international best practices defined by the INEE and UNESCO-IIEP, the MoNE made full use of the capacities it has as a huge organisation having all human resources and the production potential of vocational schools to mitigate the disaster effects. According to the research results, while the MoNE produced and distributed materials needed for food, heating and shelter for the people negatively affected in the earthquake, it also ensured that the education continued efficiently by establishing schools in tents, containers and modular buildings, by sending volunteer teachers, psychological counsellors to provide mental support to the affected students and by implementing other education-related measures as hospital classrooms for the students injured in the earthquake, to enable the students who are affected in the earthquake and have to move to other provinces to attend schools, to offer free accommodation to affected students who have to move to other cities and to provide monthly private education scholarships for affected students. As a result of the research, the MoNE implemented the measures based on the lessons learned from the COVID-19 period in a very effective way and implemented the education continuity proofs based on the evidence in the best possible way, considering the serious destruction in the earthquake affected region in Türkiye.
